UGA.label: Access to electricity
UGA.desc: Access to electricity means having an electricity source that can provide very basic lighting, and charge a phone or power a radio for 4 hours per day.
UGA.source: Data compiled from multiple sources by the World Bank – with minor processing by Our World in Data

UGA.label: GDP per capita
UGA.desc: Average economic output per person in a country or region per year. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
UGA.source: Eurostat, OECD, IMF, and World Bank (2026) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Polio vaccination rate
UGA.desc: Share of one-year-olds who have had the third dose of either the oral or inactivated polio vaccine in a given year.
UGA.source: WHO & UNICEF (2025); UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Share of women in parliament
UGA.desc: Percentage of women in the lower or single chamber of the legislature.
UGA.source: V-Dem (2026) – processed by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Primary school enrollment rate
UGA.desc: Number of children of any age group who are enrolled in primary education, expressed as a percentage of the total population of the official primary school age.
UGA.source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Total population
UGA.desc: The total population, measured on 1 July of the year shown.
UGA.source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024) – processed by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Forest cover
UGA.desc: The share of land area covered by forest, including both natural forests and forest plantations.
UGA.source: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (2013) and other sources – with major processing by Our World in Data

UGA.label: Share of population in extreme poverty
UGA.desc: Percentage of population living in households with an income or consumption below $3 per day.
UGA.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data
